items.q: Why are invasive species often more harmful than native species?
items.correct: They lack natural predators or competitors in their new environment, so they spread unchecked.
items.misconception: They are inherently more aggressive than native species.
items.random_wrong: They are larger than native species.
items.why: Students attribute success to species traits. The key factor is the ABSENCE OF CONTROLS - the same species may be ordinary in its native range, where co-evolved predators and competitors keep it in check.

items.correct: The maximum population an environment can sustainably support given available resources.
items.misconception: How much an animal can physically carry.
items.random_wrong: The size a species can reach.
items.why: Students take 'carrying' literally. It's an ECOLOGICAL concept - the ceiling set by food, water, space, etc. Populations may temporarily exceed it but then crash, or oscillate around it.

items.q: How are most microplastics formed in the environment?
items.correct: Larger plastic items break down physically into smaller fragments over time.
items.misconception: They are all manufactured at microscopic size deliberately.
items.why: Students think microplastics are designed small. Most are SECONDARY - formed when sun, waves, and wear break down larger items. Primary microplastics (microbeads, fibres) are a smaller proportion.

items.q: Crops that fix nitrogen are called:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Legumes. “Cereals” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which is NOT a feature of IPM?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Spray pesticides as first option. “Use biological control” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year did the Irish potato famine begin?
items.why: The real figure is 1845. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
